cards.text:
The useful components of garbage include two types of wastes:

 Fruit and vegetable peels, left-over cooked food and fallen leaves, can be converted into compost.

  2. Used paper, plastics, glass and metal articles that can be recycled or reused.
Biodegradable waste: The waste matter which is decomposed by microorganisms naturally is called biodegradable waste.
Non-biodegradable waste: The waste matter which is not decomposed by micro-organisms is called non-biodegradable waste.
Dustbins: The utensil or space where the waste material is collected is called a dustbin.
Green dustbins: Generally we use green dustbin for collecting biodegradable waste.
Blue dustbins: The non-biodegradable wastes which do not decompose naturally are kept in the blue dustbin.

Some of the methods of garbage disposal are:
  1. Composting
  2. Vermicomposting
  3. Recycling
  4. Reuse
  5. Landfill
Composting: The process of converting plant and animal waste materials into manure by rotting is called composting.
Landfill: Waste material, faeces of animals, kitchen waste, garden waste, etc., are collected in dustbins from where it is thrown out into the low-lying areas for filling a purpose.
Recycling: It is a process in which a material which has been used once is recycled to produce something to reuse.

Reuse: Reuse means using the same things again. The plastic jars in which we buy various food items such as jams, pickles, oils, ghee, etc. can be used later on for storing things like salt, spices, sugar, tea-leaves, etc. Example - A diary can be made from old invitation cards. Mats can be made from old clothes.
Vermicomposting: The method of preparing compost with the help of redworms is called vermicomposting.
The kitchen garbage containing fruit and vegetable peels, left-over cooked food, tea leaves, etc. are the organic wastes used in vermicomposting.
Vermicompost is high-quality manure that can be prepared by digging a pit in the ground or by using a wooden box or a metal bin.

It is better to use compost as manure instead of using chemical fertilizers because:
  1. Making compost is cheap whereas making chemical fertilizers is very expensive.
  2. Compost is natural manure that does not harm the soil but chemical fertilizers are man-made and may harm the soil in the long run.

3‘R’: We should use 3‘R’ for checking the increase of waste matter.

The first ‘R’ means ‘Reduce’.

The meaning of the second ‘R’ is ‘Reuse’ and the meaning of the third ‘R’ is ‘Recycling’.

We should use things in maximum which are biodegradable.

Reduce: We need to reduce the amount of waste generated by consuming more and throwing away less. We often buy more things than we really need. Nowadays, disposable items have become popular, for example, ballpoint pens, plastic bags, disposable napkins, etc.

Some tips for reducing waste:
  • Use a fountain pen in place of a ballpoint pen
  • Use old newspapers for packaging, and
  • Use cloth napkins in place of disposable ones.
Reuse: We can reuse certain things for more than one purpose. If we reuse them for other purposes, we can help in reducing waste.

Some of the tips for reusing things:
  • Small jars and bottles can be cleaned to keep some other kitchen stuff.
  • One should prefer a glass bottle to a metal can when buying juice or a soft drink. The bottles can be refilled.
  • Old clothes can be made into other usable items like cushion covers, handkerchief, etc.
  • Old mobile phones can be donated to friends or family.

Recycle: The process by which waste materials are used to make new products is called recycling. Materials like glass, metal, plastic, and paper are collected, separated, and recycled to make new things.
Plastics are non-biodegradable. They do not rot when buried in the soil. Only some types of plastics can be recycled. The burning of plastics produces extremely harmful gases.
